nodes.context: French-Canadian outlaw motorcycle gang based in Sherbrooke, Quebec, involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and sexual violence.
International outlaw motorcycle club that expanded into Canada, absorbing the Gitans MC as its Sherbrooke charter.
Powerful Québécois biker gang that supplied drugs and weapons to the Gitans MC and later became the first Canadian Hells Angels chapter.
Sherbrooke-based outlaw biker gang that rivaled the Gitans MC over drug turf and was eventually wiped out.
Sherbrooke street gang in the mid-1960s that evolved into the Dirty Reich MC and later the Gitans MC.
Outlaw motorcycle club that preceded the Gitans MC, using a patch with Nazi imagery.

edges.context: The Gitans MC integrated into the Hells Angels MC in December 1984 to become the Hells Angels Sherbrooke charter.
The Popeyes supplied drugs and weapons to the Gitans MC and maintained positive relations.
The Gitans and Atomes fought a violent turf war from 1973 to 1975, resulting in six murders, before reaching a truce.
In 1975, both gangs agreed to share Sherbrooke's drug market, ending the turf war.
The Popeyes became the first Canadian Hells Angels chapter (Montreal) in 1977.
The Vikings street gang evolved into the Dirty Reich MC outlaw motorcycle club.
The Dirty Reich MC rebranded as the Gitans MC in 1970, keeping the same center-patch.
